title: Mellow Creamy Chinese Suet Dumplings
description: A comforting and earthy Xinjiang dish featuring suet, perfect for a lazy Sunday, with a creamy sauce and no onions or garlic, making it friendly to all palates. This dish is a traditional Chinese recipe with a twist to accommodate sensitive tastes.

ingredients.name: suet
ingredients.notes: beef or mutton suet, finely chopped

instructions: Combine the suet, flour, and water in a large mixing bowl to form a dough.
Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a small egg.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of sesame oil in the center of each dough circle.
Add a few slices of mushrooms and a sprinkle of grated ginger on top of the oil.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Place the dumplings in a steamer lined with parchment paper, leaving some space between each dumpling.
Steam the dumplings over boiling water for 15-20 minutes, or until they are cooked through.
Meanwhile, combine the soy sauce, cream, salt, and black pepper in a small saucepan and bring to a simmer over low heat.
Serve the dumplings with the creamy sauce spooned over the top.
